questions.question: Which crop is known as 'White Gold' in Gujarat?
questions.explanation: Cotton is called 'White Gold' because of its white fluffy appearance and high economic value. Gujarat is India's largest cotton producer.

questions.question: What is the headquarters of National Dairy Development Board (NDDB)?
questions.explanation: NDDB is headquartered in Anand, Gujarat — the birthplace of the cooperative dairy movement in India.

questions.question: Which scheme provides ₹6000/year directly to farmers?
questions.explanation: PM-KISAN (Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi) provides ₹6000 per year in 3 installments directly to eligible farmer families.

questions.question: Bt Cotton was first commercially released in India in which year?
questions.explanation: Bt Cotton was first commercially approved and released in India in 2002, primarily in Gujarat, Maharashtra, and Andhra Pradesh.

questions.question: Which soil type is best for cotton cultivation?
questions.explanation: Black soil (Regur soil) is ideal for cotton as it retains moisture well and is rich in calcium carbonate, making it highly suitable.

questions.question: Kharif crops in Gujarat are sown during which period?
questions.explanation: Kharif crops are sown at the beginning of the monsoon (June) and harvested after the monsoon ends (November). E.g., cotton, groundnut, bajra.

questions.question: AMUL cooperative is located in which city of Gujarat?
questions.explanation: AMUL (Anand Milk Union Limited) is based in Anand, Gujarat. It was founded in 1946 and is managed by Gujarat Cooperative Milk Marketing Federation (GCMMF).

questions.question: Which is the major kharif oilseed crop of Gujarat?
questions.explanation: Groundnut (moongfali) is the major kharif oilseed crop in Gujarat. Gujarat produces around 40% of India's groundnut.

questions.question: The Sardar Sarovar Dam provides irrigation to approximately how many hectares?
questions.explanation: The Sardar Sarovar Dam on the Narmada River is designed to irrigate approximately 18 lakh (1.8 million) hectares of land in Gujarat.

questions.question: Which cattle breed is native to Gujarat?
questions.explanation: Gir is a famous indigenous cattle breed from the Gir forest region of Gujarat. It is known for high milk yield and is also found in Brazil.

questions.question: Gujarat was formed on which date?
questions.explanation: Gujarat was carved out from the bilingual Bombay State on 1 May 1960 based on the linguistic reorganization of states. 1 May is celebrated as Gujarat Day.

questions.question: What is the capital city of Gujarat?
questions.explanation: Gandhinagar is the capital of Gujarat, named after Mahatma Gandhi. It is one of India's planned cities and houses the state government offices.

questions.question: The Statue of Unity depicts which leader?
questions.explanation: The Statue of Unity (182m tall) is a monument to Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel, located near Kevadia, Gujarat. It is the world's tallest statue.

questions.question: Dholavira, a Harappan site, is located in which district of Gujarat?
questions.explanation: Dholavira is located on Khadir Island in the Rann of Kutch, Kutch district. It is one of the five largest Harappan cities and a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

questions.question: Rani ki Vav (stepwell) is located in which city of Gujarat?
questions.explanation: Rani ki Vav is a UNESCO World Heritage Site located in Patan, Gujarat. It was built in the 11th century by Queen Udayamati in memory of her husband King Bhimdev I.

questions.question: How many Lok Sabha seats does Gujarat have?
questions.explanation: Gujarat has 26 Lok Sabha (Parliamentary) constituencies. In Rajya Sabha, Gujarat has 11 seats.

questions.question: Surat city is famous for which industry?
questions.explanation: Surat is world-famous for diamond cutting and polishing (processes ~80% of world's diamonds) and is also a major textile hub, known as the 'Silk City'.

questions.question: Gir National Park is the only habitat for which animal?
questions.explanation: Gir National Park in Gujarat is the world's only natural habitat of the Asiatic Lion (Panthera leo persica). The lion population has significantly increased due to conservation efforts.

questions.question: Mahatma Gandhi's Dandi March (1930) started from which place?
questions.explanation: Gandhi began the Dandi March on 12 March 1930 from Sabarmati Ashram in Ahmedabad, walking 240 miles to the coastal village of Dandi to protest the British salt tax.

questions.question: GIFT City (Gujarat International Finance Tec-City) is located near which city?
questions.explanation: GIFT City is located between Ahmedabad and Gandhinagar. It is India's first operational smart city and International Financial Services Centre (IFSC).

questions.question: Who is considered the 'Aadikavi' (first poet) of Gujarati literature?
questions.explanation: Narsinh Mehta (1414–1481) is considered the 'Aadikavi' (father of Gujarati poetry). His famous bhajan 'Vaishnav Jan To' was Mahatma Gandhi's favourite.

questions.question: How many vowels (સ્વર) are there in Gujarati script?
questions.explanation: Gujarati script has 13 vowels (svar) and 36 consonants (vyanjan). It is derived from the Devanagari script but does not have the horizontal bar on top.

questions.question: Jhaverchand Meghani is known by which title?
questions.explanation: Jhaverchand Meghani (1896–1947) is called the 'Rashtreeya Shayar' (National Poet) of Gujarat. He was awarded the Ranjitram Suvarna Chandrak.

questions.question: Which language family does Gujarati belong to?
questions.explanation: Gujarati belongs to the Indo-Aryan branch of the Indo-European language family. It evolved from Sanskrit through Prakrit and Apabhramsha.

questions.question: Pannalal Patel's famous novel 'Manavini Bhavai' is set in which period?
questions.explanation: Manavini Bhavai by Pannalal Patel depicts the lives of Gujarati rural people during the devastating drought (duria) period. It won the Sahitya Akademi Award in 1985.

questions.question: How many genders does Gujarati language have?
questions.explanation: Gujarati has 3 grammatical genders: Masculine (Pulling - પુ.), Feminine (Striling - સ્ત્રી.), and Neuter (Napunsak - નપ.). This is similar to Sanskrit.

questions.question: Which of these is a Kharif crop?
questions.explanation: Bajra (Pearl Millet) is a Kharif crop grown during June-November. Wheat, Barley, and Gram are Rabi crops grown during November-April.

questions.question: The Gujarati word 'Gram Sevak' means?
questions.explanation: Gram Sevak literally means 'Village Servant/Worker'. A Gram Sevak is a government employee responsible for implementing development schemes at the village level.

questions.question: Which award is given to distinguished Gujarati literature?
questions.explanation: The Ranjitram Suvarna Chandrak is the highest literary award in Gujarat, given to distinguished Gujarati writers. It is named after Ranjitram Vavabhai Mehta.

questions.question: 'Vaishnav Jan To' was composed by which poet-saint?
questions.explanation: Vaishnav Jan To Tene Kahiye Jo Peed Parayi Jane Re was composed by Narsinh Mehta. This bhajan defines a true Vaishnav (devotee of Vishnu) as one who feels others' pain.

questions.question: Which sentence is grammatically correct?
questions.explanation: With third person singular (she/he/it), use 'doesn't' + base verb. 'She doesn't know' is the correct form.

questions.question: What is the synonym of 'Diligent'?
questions.explanation: Diligent means careful and persistent in work. Its synonym is Hardworking/Industrious. Its antonym is Lazy/Indolent.

questions.question: One Word Substitution: 'One who loves books'?
questions.explanation: Bibliophile means a person who collects or loves books. Philatelist = stamp collector, Ornithologist = bird scientist.

questions.question: Which tense is used in: 'I have completed my homework'?
questions.explanation: Present Perfect Tense = have/has + past participle. It indicates a recently completed action that has relevance to the present.

questions.question: Identify the preposition: 'The book is ON the table.'
questions.explanation: A preposition shows the relationship between a noun/pronoun and another word. 'On' shows the position of the book relative to the table.

questions.question: What does 'Omniscient' mean?
questions.explanation: Omniscient = Omni (all) + scient (knowing). It means knowing everything. God is often described as omniscient. Omnivore = eats all, Omnipotent = all powerful.

questions.question: Antonym of 'Benevolent' is?
questions.explanation: Benevolent means kind and generous. Its antonym is Malevolent (having or showing a wish to do evil). Both come from the Latin 'velle' (to wish).

questions.question: Choose the correct passive voice: 'The farmer plows the field.'
options: The field is plowed by the farmer.
The field was plowed by the farmer.
The field had been plowed by the farmer.
The field plowed by the farmer.
questions.explanation: Active: Subject + Verb + Object. Passive: Object + is/are + past participle + by + Subject. Present tense uses 'is/are' in passive.

questions.question: Which is a conjunction in: 'He is poor but honest'?
questions.explanation: A Conjunction joins words or clauses. 'But' is a coordinating conjunction showing contrast between 'poor' and 'honest'. Other conjunctions: and, or, nor, yet, so, for.

questions.question: Find the correct spelling:
questions.explanation: The correct spelling is 'Accommodation' — with double 'c' and double 'm'. It's one of the most commonly misspelled words in English.
